name: noisy_clinical_notes
description: Noisy English clinical notes with OCR artifacts for benchmarking

test_cases.description: Discharge summary with heavy OCR noise
test_cases.ground_truth: Patient presents with acute anterior wall myocardial infarction. ECG shows ST elevation in leads V1-V4. Troponin I elevated at 12.5 ng/mL. Started on dual antiplatelet therapy with aspirin 325mg and clopidogrel 600mg loading dose. Cardiology consultation requested for urgent catheterization.

test_cases.description: Chest X-ray report with moderate noise
test_cases.ground_truth: Chest X-ray demonstrates bilateral pleural effusions, more prominent on the right. Cardiomegaly with cardiothoracic ratio of 0.65. No focal consolidation. Pulmonary vascular congestion noted. Recommend clinical correlation with BNP levels.

test_cases.description: Pathology report with heavy noise and medical abbreviations
test_cases.ground_truth: Biopsy reveals well-differentiated adenocarcinoma of the colon, Grade 2 (moderately differentiated). Immunohistochemistry: CK20 positive, CDX2 positive, CK7 negative. KRAS wild-type. NRAS mutation detected. MMR proficient (MLH1, PMS2, MSH2, MSH6 retained). Tumor stage: pT3 N1 M0.

KRAS wild-lype. NRAS mulation delecled. MMR proficienl (MLH1, PMS2, MSH2, MSH6 relained). Tumor slage: pT3 N1 M0.

test_cases.ground_truth: Laparoscopic cholecystectomy performed under general anesthesia. Gallbladder distended with multiple calculi, largest measuring 1.2cm. No evidence of gallbladder wall thickening or pericholecystic fluid. Cystic artery and duct identified and clipped. Procedure completed without complications. Estimated blood loss: 50mL.

No evidence of gallbladder wall thickening or pericholecystic fluid. Cystic artery and ducldenlified and clipped. Procedure compleled without complications. Estimaied blood loss: 50mL.

test_cases.ground_truth: Well-appearing 3-year-old male presenting with 2-day history of fever up to 39.2C and productive cough. Oxygen saturation 96% on room air. Bilateral wheezing noted on auscultation. Started on albuterol nebulizer every 4 hours. Discharged with follow-up in 48 hours if symptoms persist.

test_cases.description: Neurology consultation with heavy noise and technical terms
test_cases.ground_truth: MRI brain with and without contrast reveals a 2.3 x 1.8 cm enhancing lesion in the left frontal lobe, with surrounding vasogenic edema and mild midline shift of 3mm. Differential diagnosis includes high-grade glioma (GBM) versus metastasis. Recommend stereotactic biopsy for tissue diagnosis. MRA shows no vascular abnormality.

test_cases.description: Complex polypharmacy prescription with heavy noise
test_cases.ground_truth: Take Metformin 500mg tablet orally twice daily with meals. Lisinopril 10mg once daily in the morning. Atorvastatin 40mg at bedtime. Aspirin 81mg daily for cardioprotection. Omeprazole 20mg capsule once daily before breakfast. Metoprolol succinate 50mg extended-release once daily. Follow up in 3 months with HbA1c and lipid panel.
